Prompt
U
Oracle Events
OE 1 — Search emails for Raymond Chen's QC audit findings
Search emails with a query related to "QC" or "exceptions" or "conditions cleared" or "Raymond" or "processing quality" (or similar) using search_emails to find Raymond Chen's email to Grace (subject: "QC exceptions found on 3 Tyler-cleared files") identifying the three flagged loans: LN-2026-00603 (Heather Sullivan, closed per Raymond), LN-2026-00211 (open), and LN-2026-00224 (open), along with context that Tyler had flagged workload strain and asked Elena about clearing conditions ahead of uploading docs.
OE 2 — Look up the three QC-flagged loans in the Mortgage LOS
Use mortgage_los_get_loan (loan_number: "LN-2026-00603", "LN-2026-00211", "LN-2026-00224") or mortgage_los_search_loans (query: "Sullivan" or "00603" or similar) to retrieve loan details for each flagged file. The agent discovers: LN-2026-00603 (conventional, $260,000, assigned LO Natasha Okafor, processor Rachel Kim, status processing), LN-2026-00211 (FHA, $217,000, assigned LO Brian Mitchell, processor Sofia Reyes, status withdrawn), and LN-2026-00224 (USDA, $184,000, assigned LO Marcus Webb, processor Andre Washington, status closed, closing date 2026-03-10).
OE 3 — Review conditions on the QC-flagged loans
Use mortgage_los_get_conditions for each flagged loan's internal ID (los_loan_ff737c0f7a8f, los_loan_223826af9a5d, los_loan_48275b0214f9). The agent discovers that the LOS conditions table returns no condition records for these loans, indicating that the conditions Tyler cleared were either removed or never formally entered as structured records. The agent must rely on Raymond's email narrative and the document checklists (OE 4) to understand what was flagged and what supporting docs are missing.
OE 4 — Review document checklists on the QC-flagged loans
Use mortgage_los_get_documents for each flagged loan's internal ID to verify which required documents are present, missing, or still marked as required in the checklist, confirming Raymond's findings about missing support.
OE 5 — Look up LN-2026-00556 in the Mortgage LOS
Use mortgage_los_get_loan (loan_number: "LN-2026-00556") to retrieve the file's current status (processing), assigned LO (Keisha Williams), processor (Elena Marchetti), rate lock expiration (2026-04-16), closing date (2026-04-12), loan amount ($293,600), and lender (UWM).
OE 6 — Review conditions for LN-2026-00556
Use mortgage_los_get_conditions for LN-2026-00556's internal ID (los_loan_ae236f30d53a). The LOS conditions table has no condition records for this loan. The agent must refer to the UWM kickback email from Casey Morgan (subject: "UW conditions - LN-2026-00556") to identify the 15 outstanding conditions, including items like updated pay docs, full bank statements, large deposit explanations, signed disclosures, employer start date clarification, credit inquiry LOEs, HOI info, earnest money docs, and title/contact details.
OE 7 — Search emails for Pham closing status
Search emails with a query related to "Pham" or "recording" or "wire" or "Barbara" or "closing" (or similar) using search_emails to find Barbara Hensley's emails to Tyler about docs being signed but the lender wire missing the bank cutoff for same-day recording on April 11, and Catherine Grayson's email confirming the Pham file recording was pushed to the following business day once lender funds arrive. The associated loan is LN-2026-00605 (Destiny Pham, clear_to_close, assigned LO Carlos Rivera, processor Elena Marchetti). The agent may also discover the changelog-added wire fraud alert email from Barbara to Carlos/Elena/Destiny about $47K wired to a fraudulent account.
OE 8 — Add loan activity note to LN-2026-00603
Use mortgage_los_add_activity with loan_id for LN-2026-00603, action describing QC review (e.g., "QC audit review" or similar), and detail documenting that conditions were found cleared in LOS without supporting documents in the file, noting the compliance concern given Raymond's audit identified this file as closed even though the LOS currently shows status as processing — this discrepancy itself warrants documentation.
OE 9 — Add loan activity note to LN-2026-00211
Use mortgage_los_add_activity with loan_id for LN-2026-00211, action describing QC review (or similar), and detail documenting that conditions were cleared without supporting documents and noting what corrective action is needed.
OE 10 — Add loan activity note to LN-2026-00224
Use mortgage_los_add_activity with loan_id for LN-2026-00224, action describing QC review (or similar), and detail documenting that conditions were cleared without supporting documents and noting what corrective action is needed.
OE 11 — Send email to Elena Marchetti
Use send_email with sender tyler.washington@keystonemortgage.com and recipient elena.marchetti@keystonemortgage.com to deliver a recap covering: (a) the QC audit findings across the three flagged files and their current status, (b) the LN-2026-00556 resubmission plan and outstanding UWM conditions, and (c) the Pham closing recording delay and current status uncertainty.
OE 12 — Post in the closings Slack channel about the Pham recording delay
Use conversations_add_message with channel_id C003 (#closings) to notify the team that the Pham file (LN-2026-00605) recording was delayed because the lender wire did not arrive before the bank cutoff on the scheduled closing day (April 11), that docs are fully signed, and that the current recording status needs confirmation.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 28, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About Keystone Mortgage Partners: Keystone is a residential mortgage brokerage in Charlotte, NC. The company brokers loans through wholesale lenders. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Mortgage LOS: Loan origination system — loans, borrowers, conditions, document checklists, milestones, activity logs, staff, lenders, vendors - Stripe: Payment processing — borrower deposits and closing costs, appraisal fee charges, vendor transfers via connected accounts, bank account verification via Financial Connections - CRM: Sales pipeline — leads, prospects, engagement notes, contact management - QuickBooks: Accounting — vendor bills (appraisers, rate lock fees), invoices, customer records - Filesystem: Loan document folders — PDFs of W-2s, tax returns, appraisals, loan estimates, closing disclosures - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.

OE 2 — Look up the three QC-flagged loans in the Mortgage LOS

The agent discovers: LN-2026-00603 (conventional, $260,000, assigned LO Natasha Okafor, processor Rachel Kim, status processing), LN-2026-00211 (FHA, $217,000, assigned LO Brian Mitchell, processor Sofia Reyes, status withdrawn), and LN-2026-00224 (USDA, $184,000, assigned LO Marcus Webb, processor Andre Washington, status closed, closing date 2026-03-10).

📋 Rubrics
Rubric Results
✓ Pass ✗ Fail Click any row to expand justifications
# Criterion Run #1Run #2Run #3Run #4Run #5Run #6 Pass Rate
1 Activity note added to LN-2026-00603 (Sullivan)
outcome
 ✗✗✓✗✓✓ 50%
✗Run #1The activity notes were added to los_loan_f51f37e91bd5 (LN-2025-00497), not los_loan_ff737c0f7a8f (LN-2026-00603). The agent searched for 'Sullivan' and found the wrong set of loans. No mortgage_los_add_activity call targeted the correct loan ID los_loan_ff737c0f7a8f.
✗Run #2The agent never added an activity note to LN-2026-00603 (los_loan_ff737c0f7a8f). While the agent did call mortgage_los_get_loan with 'LN-2026-00603' and retrieved the correct loan record, it then added activity notes only to the three loans returned from the Sullivan surname search (los_loan_f51f37e91bd5, los_loan_d5666464ceda, los_loan_c3c3407a19c1) which are LN-2025-00497, LN-2026-00523, and LN-2025-00459 — not the actual QC-flagged files.
✓Run #3The agent called mortgage_los_add_activity with loan_id 'los_loan_ff737c0f7a8f' (LN-2026-00603) using action 'compliance_alert', and the tool returned a successful result with a created activity record.
✗Run #4The agent added a compliance_alert activity note to los_loan_f51f37e91bd5 (LN-2025-00497, Heather Sullivan), NOT to los_loan_ff737c0f7a8f (LN-2026-00603). The agent never located or touched the correct loan ID. The agent found Sullivan-named loans via a search but missed the actual QC-flagged loans Raymond identified in his audit email.
✓Run #5The agent called mortgage_los_add_activity with loan_id 'los_loan_ff737c0f7a8f' (which corresponds to LN-2026-00603) using action 'compliance_alert'. The tool returned a successful response with an activity ID (d413f890-6f65-4a33-a91c-8f50da1d3a47), confirming the note was added.
✓Run #6The agent successfully called mortgage_los_add_activity with loan_id='los_loan_ff737c0f7a8f' (which maps to LN-2026-00603) with action='compliance_alert' and received a successful response confirming the note was created.
2 Activity note on LN-2026-00603 documents that conditions were cleared without supporting documentation
outcome
 ✗✗✓✗✓✓ 50%
✗Run #1The activity note documenting conditions cleared without supporting documents was added to los_loan_f51f37e91bd5 (LN-2025-00497), not to los_loan_ff737c0f7a8f (LN-2026-00603). The wrong loan was targeted entirely.
✗Run #2No activity note was ever added to LN-2026-00603 (los_loan_ff737c0f7a8f), so there is no note on this file documenting conditions cleared without supporting documentation.
✓Run #3The compliance_alert note on los_loan_ff737c0f7a8f explicitly states: 'Conditions in LOS show as cleared, but no supporting PDF documents are linked in the document checklist (all file_path fields are null)' — directly documenting conditions cleared without supporting documentation.
✗Run #4Although the activity note on los_loan_f51f37e91bd5 does mention conditions cleared without supporting documentation, the note was applied to the wrong loan. LN-2026-00603 (los_loan_ff737c0f7a8f) never received any activity note. Criterion requires the note on the correct loan ID.
✓Run #5The activity note detail on los_loan_ff737c0f7a8f (LN-2026-00603) explicitly states 'conditions were cleared without supporting documents uploaded' and describes missing file uploads across 14 documents, satisfying the requirement.
✓Run #6The compliance_alert note added to los_loan_ff737c0f7a8f explicitly states 'Raymond flagged this file as one of three where conditions were cleared in the LOS without supporting documents being uploaded' and details that all items marked 'received' have null file_paths, satisfying this criterion.
3 Activity note on LN-2026-00603 flags the elevated compliance risk or status discrepancy for this file
outcome
 ✗✗✓✗✗✗ 17%
✗Run #1The compliance alert note mentioning post-close risk was added to los_loan_f51f37e91bd5 (LN-2025-00497), not the required los_loan_ff737c0f7a8f (LN-2026-00603). The agent targeted the wrong loan even though it did correctly flag a closed Sullivan file as CRITICAL.
✗Run #2No activity note was added to LN-2026-00603 at all, so no elevated compliance risk or post-close exposure was documented on this specific loan in the LOS.
✓Run #3The note on los_loan_ff737c0f7a8f states: 'Raymond's finding indicates this file already closed without proper condition documentation' and instructs to 'escalate to compliance for remediation as post-close documentation gap,' directly flagging the elevated compliance risk and the status discrepancy (Raymond says closed vs. LOS showing processing).
✗Run #4The elevated compliance risk language about a closed loan with post-close exposure was included in the note on los_loan_f51f37e91bd5, but that is not LN-2026-00603 (los_loan_ff737c0f7a8f). The correct loan never received any activity note flagging compliance risk.
✗Run #5The activity note on LN-2026-00603 (los_loan_ff737c0f7a8f) treats it as a standard processing file (Janet Torres, status: Processing). It does not mention Raymond's audit claim that this file is closed, the discrepancy between Raymond's closed-status claim and the LOS showing processing, or that this file carries elevated risk compared to the other two flagged files.
✗Run #6The activity note for los_loan_ff737c0f7a8f (LN-2026-00603/Janet Torres) describes it as 'Loan status: processing (Flagstar Wholesale)' with no mention of the closed-status discrepancy, post-close compliance risk, or elevated concern vs. the other two files. The agent mistakenly attributed the post-close compliance concern to a different loan (los_loan_f51f37e91bd5/Heather Sullivan/LN-2025-00497), which it confused as the Sullivan file. The note for LN-2026-00603 lacks any indication it is the most serious QC file.
4 Activity note added to LN-2026-00211
outcome
 ✗✗✗✗✗✗ 0%
✗Run #1The activity note was added to los_loan_d5666464ceda (LN-2026-00523), not los_loan_223826af9a5d (LN-2026-00211). The agent's Sullivan search returned a different set of loans than the ones Raymond actually flagged.
✗Run #2No activity note was added to LN-2026-00211 (los_loan_223826af9a5d). The agent never looked up this loan number at all. Its activity notes went to three different Sullivan loans from a surname search.
✗Run #3The agent never added an activity note to los_loan_223826af9a5d (LN-2026-00211). The agent mistakenly treated LN-2026-00184 (los_loan_e3c96605df9f) as one of the three QC-flagged loans and added the note there instead.
✗Run #4No mortgage_los_add_activity call was made targeting los_loan_223826af9a5d (LN-2026-00211). The agent added notes to three Sullivan-named loans with different loan IDs and never located or interacted with LN-2026-00211.
✗Run #5The agent never called mortgage_los_add_activity with loan_id 'los_loan_223826af9a5d' (LN-2026-00211). The agent identified a different set of three QC-flagged loans (LN-2025-00497, LN-2026-00603, LN-2026-00523) and did not add a note to LN-2026-00211.
✗Run #6The agent never called mortgage_los_add_activity (or any tool) targeting loan_id 'los_loan_223826af9a5d' (LN-2026-00211). The agent identified and noted a different set of loans (LN-2025-00497 and LN-2026-00523) instead of the correct QC-flagged files.
5 Activity note on LN-2026-00211 documents conditions cleared without supporting docs
outcome
 ✗✗✗✗✗✗ 0%
✗Run #1The activity note was added to los_loan_d5666464ceda (LN-2026-00523), not los_loan_223826af9a5d (LN-2026-00211). While the content correctly describes conditions cleared without supporting docs, it targets the wrong loan.
✗Run #2No activity note was added to LN-2026-00211 (los_loan_223826af9a5d), so no documentation of the QC finding exists on this loan in the LOS.
